steps.name: Shared foundation: schema, db, dietary, types
criteria: createDb returns a live DbClient with WAL mode enabled and probe passes
discountItems, mealPlans, savingsLog, scrapeJobs tables defined in schema
isCompatible returns true/false per dietary restriction and tag set
steps.implementation_notes: Implement Drizzle table definitions in schema.ts (sqliteTable, text, integer columns for all tables). createDb opens better-sqlite3 in WAL mode and runs write-read-delete probe before returning — throw on probe failure. isCompatible is a pure predicate: no I/O, no side-effects. TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step MUST be written as property-based tests using fast-check @given strategies + state-delta matchers. Single-example unit tests are FALLBACK only — document why in a `# bypass:` comment if used. Integration and E2E tests REMAIN single-example — they verify wiring, not invariants.

steps.name: Scraping pipeline and discount registration
criteria: FakeAldiCatalogueAdapter.fromFixtureFile reads fixture JSON and fetchCurrentWeek returns items
CatalogueNormalizer.normalize filters items missing both price and discountedPrice (D21)
CatalogueNormalizer.normalize maps price strings to integer cents and applies dietaryTags
scraper-runner exits 0 when CATALOGUE_SOURCE=fake and fixture contains valid items
Normalised items are stored via DiscountService.registerDiscountItem
steps.scenario_name: Walking Skeleton — Scenario 1: Shopper sees discounted items, generates a meal plan, and confirms savings match the estimate
steps.implementation_notes: ScrapingService.run orchestrates: start job, fetch, normalize, registerDiscountItem for each item, complete job. scraper-runner reads CATALOGUE_SOURCE env var to select adapter: FakeAldiCatalogueAdapter.fromFixtureFile(FAKE_CATALOGUE_FIXTURE) or live fetcher. CatalogueNormalizer contract shape: pure-function / return-only — no DB writes, no I/O. DiscountService.registerDiscountItem is the only impure function in this boundary (writes to sqlite_discount_item_repository). D21: normalizer MUST drop items where discountedPrice is absent or empty. D22: regularPrice is written once; the repository must not allow updates. TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step MUST be written as property-based tests using fast-check @given strategies + state-delta matchers. Single-example unit tests are FALLBACK only — document why in a `# bypass:` comment if used. Integration and E2E tests REMAIN single-example — they verify wiring, not invariants.

steps.name: Discount HTTP feed: GET /
criteria: GET / returns 200 HTML containing item names and both regular and sale prices
Items missing either price are absent from the feed (D21 filter visible in output)
steps.scenario_name: Walking Skeleton — Scenario 1: Shopper sees discounted items, generates a meal plan, and confirms savings match the estimate
steps.implementation_notes: DiscountHandler.handleGet calls DiscountService.getWeeklyItems and renders HTMX HTML with item name, regular price (was €X.XX), and sale price. Handler is a thin driving adapter — no business logic, no DB access directly. Contract shape: bounded-change (reads discount_items, no writes). TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step MUST be written as property-based tests using fast-check @given strategies + state-delta matchers. Single-example unit tests are FALLBACK only — document why in a `# bypass:` comment if used. Integration and E2E tests REMAIN single-example — they verify wiring, not invariants.

steps.name: Plan generation and savings tracking (D23 atomic)
criteria: POST /plan/generate returns 200 and GET /plan renders data-estimated-savings attribute in cents
GET /savings renders data-saved-amount attribute equal to data-estimated-savings (D23)
PlanService.savePlan writes meal_plans and savings_log in a single SQLite transaction
steps.scenario_name: Walking Skeleton — Scenario 1: Shopper sees discounted items, generates a meal plan, and confirms savings match the estimate
steps.implementation_notes: PlanService.generatePlan is a pure function (plan-value pattern): takes weekStart + prefs, reads discount items via DiscountService, returns a MealPlan value — no DB writes. PlanService.savePlan is the ONLY impure function: opens a single SQLite transaction, writes meal_plans.estimated_savings and savings_log.saved_amount atomically — the D23 invariant means partial writes are structurally impossible. plan-handler renders estimated_savings as `data-estimated-savings="{cents}"`. savings-handler renders saved_amount as `data-saved-amount="{cents}"`. SavingsService.recordSavings is called by PlanService.savePlan inside the same tx — NOT via HTTP. TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step MUST be written as property-based tests using fast-check @given strategies + state-delta matchers. Single-example unit tests are FALLBACK only — document why in a `# bypass:` comment if used. Integration and E2E tests REMAIN single-example — they verify wiring, not invariants.

steps.name: Server composition root (D35 wire order)
criteria: createServer wires all services and routes in order: createDb, services, probe, routes
Server starts and acceptance test suite passes green end-to-end
steps.scenario_name: Walking Skeleton — Scenario 1: Shopper sees discounted items, generates a meal plan, and confirms savings match the estimate
steps.implementation_notes: createServer follows D35 wire order: (1) createDb(dbPath), (2) instantiate all SQLite adapters and services, (3) run probe — emit health.startup.refused and exit 1 on failure, (4) register routes: GET / → DiscountHandler, POST /plan/generate and GET /plan → PlanHandler, GET /savings → SavingsHandler. Returns ServerHandle with stop(). When CATALOGUE_SOURCE=fake the server does not wire the scraping pipeline — scraper-runner.ts is the separate entry point. TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step MUST be written as property-based tests using fast-check @given strategies + state-delta matchers. Single-example unit tests are FALLBACK only — document why in a `# bypass:` comment if used. Integration and E2E tests REMAIN single-example — they verify wiring, not invariants.

phases.name: Slice-02: Real Scrapers, 7-Day Meal Plan, Staleness Warning, Empty-Store UI

steps.name: Real AldiSudCatalogueFetcher
criteria: Given a live prospekt.aldi-sued.de endpoint, when AldiSudCatalogueFetcher.fetchCurrentWeek() is called, then it returns only items of type 'product' that have both a price and a discountedPrice where discountedPrice < price
Given a 302 Location header of '//prospekt.aldi-sued.de/kw27-26-op-mp/', when the slug is parsed, then the result is 'kw27-26-op-mp'
Given pages 1 through N exist and page N+1 returns 404, when the fetcher paginates, then it stops after the 404 and returns items from all preceding pages

Given a hotspot entry with type 'product' and discountedPrice present and parseFloat(discountedPrice) < parseFloat(price), when fetchCurrentWeek() returns, then the entry is included in the result array
Given a hotspot entry with type 'banner' or with discountedPrice absent or discountedPrice >= price, when fetchCurrentWeek() returns, then the entry is excluded from the result array
Given a network error on the HEAD request, when fetchCurrentWeek() is called, then the promise rejects with a structured error
steps.scenario_name: AldiSudCatalogueFetcher — slug parsing and item filtering
steps.implementation_notes:
TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step MUST be written as property-based tests using fast-check @given strategies + state-delta matchers. Single-example unit tests are FALLBACK only — document why in a `# bypass:` comment if used. Integration and E2E tests REMAIN single-example — they verify wiring, not invariants.

Remove the `__SCAFFOLD__ = true` constant and implement the three methods.

`discoverSlug()`: issue a HEAD request to `https://prospekt.aldi-sued.de/`. Read the `Location` response header. Parse the slug using a regex against the pattern `//prospekt.aldi-sued.de/([^/]+)/`. Throw a structured error if the header is absent or does not match.

`fetchPage(slug, page)`: GET `https://prospekt.aldi-sued.de/${slug}/page/${page}-${page+1}/hotspots_data.json`. Return parsed JSON array on 200. Return empty array `[]` on 404 (pagination sentinel). Throw on any other status.

`fetchCurrentWeek()`: call `discoverSlug()`, then loop `fetchPage()` starting at page 1, incrementing by 1, stopping when `fetchPage` returns `[]`. Flatten all results. Filter: keep only entries where `type === 'product'` AND `discountedPrice` is a non-empty string AND `parseFloat(discountedPrice) < parseFloat(price)`. Return filtered array typed as `{id, title, brand, price, discountedPrice, customLabel1, productType, photoUrls}[]`.

Test approach: use fast-check arbitraries to generate slug strings matching `kw{N}-{yy}-op-mp` and assert the regex always extracts the slug portion correctly (property test). Stub `globalThis.fetch` or inject a `fetch` parameter into the constructor for HTTP interaction tests (fixture-based single example with bypass comment). The `FakeAldiCatalogueAdapter` continues to serve all acceptance tests — no AT change needed for this step.

steps.name: CatalogueExtractor port + HaikuCatalogueExtractor + VMarktCatalogueFetcher
criteria: Given a set of German supermarket paragraph strings, when HaikuCatalogueExtractor.extractProducts() is called, then it returns an array of objects each with name (string), regularPrice (EUR string), and salePrice (EUR string)

Given paragraph input where salePrice >= regularPrice for an extracted entry, when VMarktCatalogueFetcher.fetchCurrentWeek() processes the extraction result, then that entry is excluded from the returned items

Given a VMarktCatalogueFetcher constructed with a FakeCatalogueExtractor, when fetchCurrentWeek() is called, then it does not call the Anthropic API and returns items using the extractor's fixture output

Given a slug page at pageflip.v-markt.de/muenchen/{slug}/ containing paragraph HTML, when VMarktCatalogueFetcher.fetchCurrentWeek() is called, then the paragraph text blocks are passed to CatalogueExtractor.extractProducts()
Given the slug discovery page at v-markt.de/angebote/muenchen returns multiple hrefs, when the fetcher extracts slugs matching /^\d{4}_VMMUC$/, then it selects the lexicographically latest one

Given an extracted product with valid salePrice < regularPrice, when VMarktCatalogueFetcher.fetchCurrentWeek() returns, then the item shape includes id, title, brand, price, discountedPrice, customLabel1, productType, and photoUrls so it is compatible with CatalogueNormalizer
steps.scenario_name: VMarktCatalogueFetcher — slug discovery, extraction delegation, and price filtering
steps.implementation_notes:
TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step MUST be written as property-based tests using fast-check @given strategies + state-delta matchers. Single-example unit tests are FALLBACK only — document why in a `# bypass:` comment if used. Integration and E2E tests REMAIN single-example — they verify wiring, not invariants. **Port interface** `src/scraping/adapters/catalogue-extractor.ts`: define and export `interface CatalogueExtractor { extractProducts(paragraphs: string[]): Promise> }`. No implementation here. **HaikuCatalogueExtractor** `src/scraping/adapters/haiku-catalogue-extractor.ts`: import `Anthropic` from `@anthropic-ai/sdk`. Constructor reads `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` from environment. Implements `CatalogueExtractor`. The `extractProducts()` method sends the paragraphs array to `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` with a prompt instructing the model to extract German supermarket product rows as JSON: `[{name, regularPrice, salePrice}]` where prices are EUR strings like `'1.29'`. Parse the response JSON and return the array. Never mutate state — this is a bounded-change adapter (reads env, calls external API, returns value). **VMarktCatalogueFetcher** `src/scraping/adapters/v-markt-catalogue-fetcher.ts`: implements the same `fetchCurrentWeek(): Promise` shape as `AldiSudCatalogueFetcher`. Constructor takes `extractor: CatalogueExtractor` and optionally a fetch injectable. Slug discovery: GET `https://www.v-markt.de/angebote/muenchen`, extract all hrefs matching `pageflip.v-markt.de/muenchen/`, collect path segments matching `/^\d{4}_VMMUC$/`, pick the lexicographically latest. Catalogue fetch: GET `https://www.pageflip.v-markt.de/muenchen/{slug}/`, extract all `

` text content blocks. Call `extractor.extractProducts(paragraphs)`. Filter: keep only entries where `parseFloat(salePrice) < parseFloat(regularPrice)`. Map each valid entry to a `CatalogueItem`-compatible shape: set `id` as a generated UUID or `${slug}-${index}`, `title` = name, `brand` = 'V-Markt', `price` = regularPrice, `discountedPrice` = salePrice, `customLabel1` as current ISO date, `productType` = 'grocery', `photoUrls` = []. **CatalogueNormalizer**: the existing `normalize()` method already handles the `CatalogueItem` shape and is store-agnostic. No structural change needed — VMarktCatalogueFetcher maps to the same shape before returning, so CatalogueNormalizer requires no new branch. If a minor guard is needed (e.g., V-Markt dairy tags), extend `classifyDietaryTags()` with a 'dairy' case. **FakeCatalogueExtractor** `tests/acceptance/support/fake-catalogue-extractor.ts`: implement `CatalogueExtractor` returning a hardcoded fixture array of `{name, regularPrice, salePrice}` objects. Used in unit tests to avoid Anthropic API calls. Constructor accepts optional fixture data override.

steps.name: Live scraper wiring in scraper-runner.ts
criteria: Given CATALOGUE_SOURCE=live and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set, when the scraper runner is executed, then it wires AldiSudCatalogueFetcher and VMarktCatalogueFetcher with HaikuCatalogueExtractor and calls ScrapingService.run() for both stores
Given CATALOGUE_SOURCE=live and all HTTP calls succeed, when the runner exits, then the process exits with code 0
Given CATALOGUE_SOURCE=live and AldiSudCatalogueFetcher throws, when the runner handles the error, then the process exits with code 1 and logs a structured error event
Given CATALOGUE_SOURCE=fake (unchanged), when the runner executes, then it continues to use FakeAldiCatalogueAdapter and FakeVMarktCatalogueAdapter exactly as in Slice-01
steps.scenario_name: scraper-runner — live mode wiring and exit codes
steps.implementation_notes:
TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step MUST be written as property-based tests using fast-check @given strategies + state-delta matchers. Single-example unit tests are FALLBACK only — document why in a `# bypass:` comment if used. Integration and E2E tests REMAIN single-example — they verify wiring, not invariants.

Extend the `main()` function: when `source === 'live'`, check for `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` env var (throw `health.scrape.refused` if absent). Instantiate `AldiSudCatalogueFetcher` and `VMarktCatalogueFetcher` with `new HaikuCatalogueExtractor()`. Run both via `ScrapingService.run('Aldi Süd')` and `ScrapingService.run('V-Markt')`. The existing `process.exit(0)` on success and `process.exit(1)` on error in the `.catch()` block remain unchanged.

The `fake` branch must remain fully intact — acceptance tests use `CATALOGUE_SOURCE=fake`. No changes to the fake branch logic.

Unit test strategy: inject stub fetcher and stub scraping service via a testable factory function extracted from `main()`. Test that when source='live' and fetchers succeed, the runner resolves without throwing. Test that when a fetcher rejects, the runner rejects. Mark as single-example with `# bypass: wiring test verifies composition, not invariants` comment. CI can skip this test if `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is not set (guard with `test.skipIf`).

steps.name: 7-day meal plan generation
criteria: Given a non-empty list of discount items, when POST /plan/generate is called and GET /plan is fetched, then the plan HTML contains exactly 14 meal entries (7 days x 2 slots: lunch and dinner)
Given fewer than 14 discount items, when generatePlan() is called, then items are cycled so all 14 meal slots are filled (no slot is empty)
Given 0 discount items, when generatePlan() is called, then all 14 meals have discountItemId null and a generic placeholder name
Given a generated plan, when GET /plan is rendered, then each meal entry shows the day number (1-7), the slot (lunch or dinner), and the item name or placeholder
Given the D23 invariant, when POST /plan/generate completes and GET /savings is fetched, then the saved_amount data attribute equals the estimated_savings data attribute from GET /plan
Given a plan already exists for the current week, when POST /plan/generate is called again, then no new plan is created and the existing plan is returned (idempotency preserved)
steps.scenario_name: Walking Skeleton — Scenario 1: Shopper sees discounted items, generates a meal plan, and confirms savings match the estimate
steps.implementation_notes:
TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step MUST be written as property-based tests using fast-check @given strategies + state-delta matchers. Single-example unit tests are FALLBACK only — document why in a `# bypass:` comment if used. Integration and E2E tests REMAIN single-example — they verify wiring, not invariants.

**Types** (`src/shared/types.ts`): add `type MealSlot = 'lunch' | 'dinner'` and `interface Meal { day: number; slot: MealSlot; name: string; discountItemId: string | null }`.

**Schema** (`src/shared/schema.ts`): add `meals: text('meals').notNull().default('[]')` column to the `mealPlans` table definition. The column stores a JSON array of `Meal` objects.

**Repository** (`src/meal-planning/adapters/sqlite-meal-plan-repository.ts`): extend the `MealPlan` interface with `meals: Meal[]`. Update `save()` to serialize `meals` as JSON. Update `findByWeek()` to deserialize `meals` from JSON on read.

**PlanService** (`src/meal-planning/plan-service.ts`): update `generatePlan()` — pure computation, no DB writes. Build 14 meals by iterating days 1-7 and slots ['lunch','dinner']. For each slot index (0-13), if `discountItems.length > 0`, pick `discountItems[index % discountItems.length]` and set `discountItemId = item.id` and `name = item.name`. If `discountItems.length === 0`, set `discountItemId = null` and `name = 'No discount items this week'`. Attach `meals` array to the returned `MealPlan` object. `estimatedSavings` calculation is unchanged.

**PlanHandler** (`src/meal-planning/http/plan-handler.ts`): update `renderPlanHtml()` to render the 7-day grid. Render a table or list with rows for each day (Mon-Sun labels) and columns for lunch and dinner. Each cell shows the meal name. The `data-estimated-savings` attribute contract is preserved unchanged.

**Acceptance test extension** (`tests/acceptance/discount-hunt/walking-skeleton.test.ts`): in Scenario 1, after fetching `GET /plan`, assert that the plan HTML contains 14 meal entries. Use a CSS selector count approach: `(html.match(/data-meal-slot/g) ?? []).length === 14` or assert the presence of 14 `<tr class="meal-row">` elements. Add `data-meal-slot` or `data-day` attributes to each meal row in the template for machine-readability. The D23 invariant test (`estimated_savings === saved_amount`) is unchanged.

Unit test for `generatePlan()` (property-based): use fast-check to generate arrays of 0-20 discount items and assert that the result always has exactly 14 meals, each with a valid day (1-7) and slot ('lunch'|'dinner'), and that when items > 0, all discountItemIds are non-null.

steps.name: Staleness warning + per-store empty-state UI
criteria: Given a scrape_jobs row for 'Aldi Süd' with completed_at = now - 72 hours, when GET / is fetched, then the HTML contains a staleness warning element with text referencing 'Aldi Süd' and the last refresh date
Given a scrape_jobs row for 'V-Markt' with completed_at = now - 24 hours (within 48h threshold), when GET / is fetched, then no staleness warning appears for V-Markt
Given a store that has had at least one scrape job but 0 discount items this week, when GET / is fetched, then the HTML shows 'No discounts this week at {store}' inside that store's section
Given no scrape jobs exist for any store, when GET / is fetched, then the global 'No discounts available this week' message is shown and no per-store section is rendered
Given discount items exist for both stores, when GET / is fetched, then each store's items appear under a per-store section heading with no empty-state message for that store
Given two stores have had scrape jobs and one is stale, when GET / is fetched, then the staleness banner appears only for the stale store and the fresh store renders normally
steps.scenario_name: @walking_skeleton @driving_port — Shopper sees staleness warning and per-store empty state
steps.implementation_notes:
TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step MUST be written as property-based tests using fast-check @given strategies + state-delta matchers. Single-example unit tests are FALLBACK only — document why in a `# bypass:` comment if used. Integration and E2E tests REMAIN single-example — they verify wiring, not invariants. **Repository** (`src/scraping/adapters/sqlite-scrape-job-repository.ts`): add method `getLastSuccessfulRunByStore(store: string): number | null`. Query `scrape_jobs` where `store = ?` AND `status = 'completed'`, order by `completed_at DESC`, limit 1. Return `completedAt` as a millisecond timestamp, or `null` if no row found. This is a read-only query — contract shape: pure-function / return-only. Also add `getStoresWithJobs(): string[]` to return distinct store names that have at least one scrape job row (used by DiscountHandler to determine which per-store sections to render). **DiscountHandler** (`src/discount/http/discount-handler.ts`): update constructor to accept `scrapeJobRepository: SQLiteScrapeJobRepository` as a second argument. In `handleGet()`, after fetching `items`, call `scrapeJobRepository.getStoresWithJobs()` to get the list of stores that have ever been scraped. For each known store, call `scrapeJobRepository.getLastSuccessfulRunByStore(store)`. If `completedAt !== null` and `Date.now() - completedAt > 48 * 60 * 60 * 1000`, render a `
Data for {store} may be outdated — last refreshed {date}
` banner at the top of the response. For item grouping: if `getStoresWithJobs()` returns stores but a store has 0 items in `items`, still render its `
` but populate it with `

No discounts this week at {store}

` instead of item cards. If no stores have jobs at all, render the global `

No discounts available this week

` as before (backward-compatible fallback). **Server** (`src/server.ts`): instantiate `SQLiteScrapeJobRepository` and inject it into `new DiscountHandler(discountService, scrapeJobRepo)`. **Acceptance test extension** (`tests/acceptance/discount-hunt/multi-store.test.ts`): add a new `describe` block for the staleness scenario. In `beforeAll`, seed a `scrape_jobs` row directly into the test DB using drizzle (or raw SQL) with `store = 'Aldi Süd'`, `status = 'completed'`, `completed_at = Date.now() - 72 * 60 * 60 * 1000`. Start server. Assert `GET /` HTML contains `class="staleness-warning"` and the text `'Aldi Süd'`. Assert the warning does NOT appear when completed_at is within 48h. Unit test for staleness threshold (property-based): use fast-check to generate arbitrary `completedAt` timestamps and assert that the staleness predicate `now - completedAt > 48h` is a pure boolean function with no side effects. Use state-delta matcher: input timestamp in → boolean out, no mutations.

steps.name: Post-verification data correctness: week filter + DB migration guard
criteria: Given discount items from a prior week (validUntil < weekStart), when getByWeek(weekStart) is called, then those items are NOT returned
Given discount items valid during the current week (validUntil >= weekStart), when getByWeek(weekStart) is called, then those items ARE returned
Given an existing discount-hunt.db created before the meals column was added, when createDb() is called, then the meals column is present and the app starts without error
steps.scenario_name: Walking Skeleton — Scenario 1
steps.implementation_notes:
Two bug fixes found during /verify:

1. getByWeek() currently does a full table scan ignoring weekStart. Fix: add a WHERE clause `valid_until >= weekStart` using Drizzle's gte() operator (import from drizzle-orm). This filters out items whose validUntil date is before the requested week started.

2. createDb() uses CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS which does not add new columns to existing databases. Fix: after the CREATE TABLE statements, run `ALTER TABLE meal_plans ADD COLUMN meals TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'` wrapped in a try/catch — SQLite throws if the column already exists, which is the normal case for fresh databases.

TEST PARADIGM: Unit/property-based test for the week filter invariant: for any item where validUntil < weekStart, it must be absent from the result; for validUntil >= weekStart, it must be present.

steps.name: Regression ATs: prior-week filter + schema migration boot
criteria: Given discount items seeded with validUntil in the previous week, when GET / is fetched, then those items do NOT appear in the HTML
Given discount items seeded with validUntil in the current or future week, when GET / is fetched, then those items DO appear in the HTML
Given a SQLite file DB created without the meals column, when createServer() is called, then the server starts without error and POST /plan/generate returns a non-error response
steps.scenario_name: Regression: prior-week items excluded + schema migration boot
steps.implementation_notes:
Add two new describe blocks to multi-store.test.ts:

**Block 1 — prior-week filter**:
- Compute current week Monday using the same formula as discount-handler (getUTCDay → daysToMonday). Also compute a past date (10 days ago) that is definitely before the current Monday.
- Seed two scrape_jobs rows (one for Aldi Süd, one for V-Markt) and insert discount_items directly via the DB client (same pattern as staleness block). Two items with validUntil = pastDate (last week), two items with validUntil = futureDate (7 days from now).
- Start the server.
- GET / → assert past-week items (by name) are ABSENT. Assert future-week items ARE present.
- No scraper subprocess needed — insert via db.insert(discountItems) directly.

**Block 2 — schema migration boot**:
- Open a raw bun:sqlite Database, create meal_plans WITHOUT the meals column (use `CREATE TABLE meal_plans (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, week_start TEXT NOT NULL, item_ids TEXT NOT NULL, total_regular_price INTEGER NOT NULL, total_sale_price INTEGER NOT NULL, estimated_savings INTEGER NOT NULL, created_at INTEGER NOT NULL)`), close it.
- Call createServer() against this DB path.
- Assert server starts without throwing.
- POST /plan/generate → assert response is not a 500 (accepts 303 redirect or 200).
- Confirms the try/catch ALTER TABLE in createDb() handles pre-meals DBs.

Use bun:sqlite's Database directly for the schema migration test (not createDb) so you can create the old schema without the meals column. Import: `import { Database } from 'bun:sqlite'`.

phases.name: Slice-03 increment 1: dietary preference (Settings page + live filter + plan snapshot)

steps.name: Schema foundation: user_settings singleton + meal_plans.dietary_filter snapshot column
criteria: Given a fresh database, when createDb() runs, then a user_settings table exists with columns user_id (TEXT PK DEFAULT 'dimitar'), dietary_restriction (TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'none'), updated_at (INTEGER NOT NULL)
Given a fresh database, when createDb() runs, then meal_plans has a dietary_filter column (TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'none')

Given a pre-existing meal_plans table created before the dietary_filter column, when createDb() runs, then the column is added via a guarded ALTER TABLE and the app starts without error (mirrors the meals-column guard at db.ts:87-92)
Given the MealPlan type, when extended, then it carries a dietaryFilter field so the snapshotted restriction round-trips through save/load
steps.scenario_name: @driving_port — Saving a dietary restriction confirms and pre-fills on reload
steps.implementation_notes:
Foundation step for SLICE-03 increment 1 (design-preferences-model.md §1). This step ships no user-visible behavior alone; it is a prerequisite for scenario 2 (persistence) going green.

**schema.ts**: add the Drizzle `userSettings` sqliteTable — userId text PK with default 'dimitar', dietaryRestriction text NOT NULL default 'none', updatedAt integer NOT NULL. Add `dietaryFilter: text('dietary_filter').notNull().default('none')` to the existing mealPlans table definition.

**db.ts**: add a `CREATE_USER_SETTINGS` constant (`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_settings (user_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT 'dimitar', dietary_restriction TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'none', updated_at INTEGER NOT NULL)`) and exec it in createDb() alongside the other CREATE_* statements. Add `dietary_filter TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'none'` to the CREATE_MEAL_PLANS constant (fresh DBs). Add a guarded idempotent migration for pre-existing DBs, EXACTLY mirroring the meals guard at db.ts:87-92: `try { sqlite.exec("ALTER TABLE meal_plans ADD COLUMN dietary_filter TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'none'"); } catch {}`. The user_settings singleton is guaranteed by the fixed PK 'dimitar' + upsert-on-conflict in the adapter (03-02), not by db.ts.

**types.ts**: add the `UserPreferences` interface with a single `dietaryRestriction: DietaryRestriction` field (single enum 'none'|'vegetarian'|'vegan', NOT an array — design §8 INFO #6 reconciliation, v1 is single-value).

**sqlite-meal-plan-repository.ts**: extend the `MealPlan` interface with `dietaryFilter: DietaryRestriction`; map the new column in save() (write) and findByWeek() (read) so the snapshot round-trips. Contract shape: bounded-change (reads/writes meal_plans; the dietary_filter value is snapshotted, never rewritten after save — design §5 D25).

TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step MUST be written as property-based tests using fast-check @given strategies + state-delta matchers over a declared universe with strict=true. Single-example unit tests are FALLBACK only — document why in a `# bypass:` comment if used. The schema-migration boot (pre-existing DB without dietary_filter → app starts) is a single-example integration test (mirror multi-store.test.ts Block 2 for the meals column). Integration and E2E tests REMAIN single-example — they verify wiring, not invariants.

steps.name: Preferences context: repository port + SQLite singleton adapter + service
criteria: Given no user_settings row exists, when UserPreferencesRepository.get() is called, then it returns the honest default { dietaryRestriction: 'none' } (never null)
Given a dietary restriction is upserted, when get() is called afterward, then it returns the persisted value
Given upsert() is called twice, when the table is inspected, then it still holds exactly one row (fixed-PK 'dimitar' + INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(user_id) DO UPDATE — the singleton cannot grow)
Given a POST-then-GET round trip through the driving port, when the restriction is saved then re-read, then the saved value is returned (persistence, backs scenario 2)
steps.scenario_name: @driving_port — Saving a dietary restriction confirms and pre-fills on reload
steps.implementation_notes:
New `src/preferences/` bounded context (design §3, Generic subdomain, no domain logic).

**ports/preferences-repository.ts**: define and export `interface UserPreferencesRepository { get(): UserPreferences; upsert(prefs: UserPreferences): void }`. get() returns defaults if the row is unset (never null) — consumers never branch on 'is there a row'; the port hides single-row bootstrap. This is a genuine read+write CRUD port (settings page writes; plan-service and discount-handler read) — a single port is correct because the settings handler legitimately needs both (design §3 Port shape note; read/write split NOT required here).

**adapters/sqlite-user-preferences-repository.ts**: implements the port over the shared DbClient. get() selects the single row by user_id='dimitar', returning { dietaryRestriction: 'none' } when absent. upsert() writes with the fixed PK using `INSERT INTO user_settings (user_id, dietary_restriction, updated_at) VALUES ('dimitar', ?, ?) ON CONFLICT(user_id) DO UPDATE SET dietary_restriction=excluded.dietary_restriction, updated_at=excluded.updated_at` — a second write updates the same row, so the table structurally cannot hold >1 settings row. Sets updated_at to Date.now() on every upsert. Substrate probe (Principle 13): NO new probe — this adapter inherits the shared WAL write-read-delete startup probe in db.ts (design §3); the composition-root wire→probe→use invariant (D35) already covers it via the shared DB client.

**preferences-service.ts**: thin CRUD wrapper — `getPreferences(): UserPreferences` and `updatePreferences(prefs): void` delegating to the repository. No domain logic.

TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step MUST be written as property-based tests using fast-check @given strategies + state-delta matchers over a declared universe with strict=true. Property for the singleton invariant: for any sequence of upserts with arbitrary restrictions, the row count stays exactly 1 and get() returns the last-written value. Property for get()-default: with no row, get() always returns { dietaryRestriction: 'none' }. Single-example unit tests are FALLBACK only — document why in a `# bypass:` comment if used. Integration and E2E tests REMAIN single-example — they verify wiring, not invariants.

steps.name: Settings handler + /settings route + Preferences page (GET pre-filled, POST upsert)
criteria: Given GET /settings, when fetched, then it returns 200 HTML with a <select name="dietary"> offering None, Vegetarian, and Vegan options

Given a previously-saved restriction (e.g. vegetarian), when GET /settings is fetched, then the matching <option value="vegetarian"> carries the selected attribute (pre-fill sourced live from PreferencesRepository.get())
Given POST /settings with dietary=vegetarian (x-www-form-urlencoded), when submitted, then the row is upserted and the response contains the text 'Settings saved'
Given a POST /settings followed by a GET /settings, when re-read, then the just-saved value is pre-selected
steps.scenario_name: @driving_port — Settings page shows the current dietary restriction pre-selected
steps.implementation_notes:
Satisfies acceptance scenarios 1 (@driving_port — Settings page shows the current dietary restriction pre-selected) AND 2 (@driving_port — Saving a dietary restriction confirms and pre-fills on reload).

**http/settings-handler.ts**: thin driving adapter (no business logic; no direct DB). `handleGet()` reads PreferencesService.getPreferences() and server-renders (D31) a general 'Preferences' page — one field per LIVE dimension only, which in increment 1 is exactly one: a dietary dropdown `<select name="dietary">` with options None/Vegetarian/Vegan, current value pre-selected via the `selected` attribute (design §4). No control for budget/household/kid-friendly — an inert control is testing theater (design §0). `handlePost()` parses the x-www-form-urlencoded `dietary` field, calls PreferencesService.updatePreferences(...), then re-renders the page with a 'Settings saved' confirmation banner and the just-saved value pre-selected. Note: the AT for scenario 2 asserts `response.ok` on POST (a 200 with the page + 'Settings saved'), so POST returns the rendered page, not a 303 redirect.

**server.ts** (composition root, D35 wire order): instantiate SQLiteUserPreferencesRepository → PreferencesService → SettingsHandler; register `GET /settings` and `POST /settings` routing to the handler. Do NOT modify plan-service/discount-handler wiring here — that is 03-04/03-05.

EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: this step is HTTP route registration + server-rendered HTML (a single-shot UI flow with exact-string assertions 'Settings saved' and a `selected` option). # bypass: wiring test verifies composition and rendered output, not invariants. Compensate with the property-based singleton/persistence test at the unit level in 03-02. Integration/E2E (the two acceptance scenarios) REMAIN single-example.

steps.name: Dashboard live filter: un-hardcode discount-handler restriction read
criteria: Given vegetarian is saved, when GET / is fetched, then the dashboard shows zero items tagged contains-meat/contains-fish immediately, with no regenerate step (live read)
Given vegan is saved, when GET / is fetched, then only the vegan-tagged item appears (vegetarian-only item is also filtered out)
Given the restriction is set back to none, when GET / is fetched, then all items including meat and fish reappear on the next load (anti-Fixture-Theater control proving the items exist)
steps.scenario_name: @driving_port — Discount dashboard re-filters live when the restriction changes
steps.implementation_notes:
Satisfies acceptance scenario 3 (@driving_port — Discount dashboard re-filters live when the restriction changes). Design §2.1 (LIVE dashboard effect) + §6 dashboard-filter row.

**discount-handler.ts**: inject UserPreferencesRepository into the constructor. In handleGet(), REPLACE the hardcoded `this.discountService.getWeeklyItems(weekStart, "none")` at discount-handler.ts:50 with a LIVE read: `const { dietaryRestriction } = this.preferencesRepository.get();` then `getWeeklyItems(weekStart, dietaryRestriction)`. The repo is read on EVERY request — no caching — so the feed re-filters the moment the setting changes (getByWeek already applies isCompatible() as an unconditional pre-filter, D33). Contract shape: bounded-change (reads discount_items + user_settings, no writes).

**server.ts**: pass the already-wired SQLiteUserPreferencesRepository (from 03-03) into `new DiscountHandler(discountService, scrapeJobRepo, preferencesRepository)`.

Deps: 03-03 because the scenario-3 beforeAll/tests call POST /settings (404 without the route); 03-02 for the repository. This step depends on 03-03 wiring existing, not on modifying the settings handler.

EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: this is a one-line un-hardcoding + constructor injection; its observable is the rendered dashboard HTML (single-shot flow, exact item-name presence/absence). # bypass: wiring test verifies composition, not invariants. isCompatible() is already property-covered at the unit level (step 01-01). Integration/E2E (scenario 3) REMAINS single-example.

steps.name: Plan generation: un-hardcode restriction, snapshot dietary_filter onto the plan
criteria: Given vegetarian is saved and a plan is generated, when GET /plan is fetched, then no meal references a contains-meat or contains-fish item, and at least one vegetarian/vegan-compatible meal remains (filtered, not emptied)

Given a vegetarian plan was generated then the setting is switched to none, when GET /plan is fetched, then the existing plan still shows zero meat/fish (its dietary_filter is frozen at 'vegetarian' — snapshot immutability, D25)

Given the same setup, when GET / (dashboard, live) is fetched alongside GET /plan (snapshot), then the dashboard shows meat again while the plan still hides it — proving the plan is frozen, not coincidental
steps.scenario_name: @driving_port — An existing vegetarian plan stays meat-free after the setting changes to none
steps.implementation_notes:
Satisfies acceptance scenarios 4 (@driving_port — Generated meal plan excludes meat/fish under a vegetarian restriction) AND 5 (@driving_port — An existing vegetarian plan stays meat-free after the setting changes to none). Design §2.1 (generation/snapshot) + §5 (snapshot vs live) + §6 plan-filter and snapshot-immutability rows.

**plan-service.ts**: inject UserPreferencesRepository. Extend `generatePlan(weekStart, items)` → `generatePlan(weekStart, items, dietaryFilter)` — still a PURE function (plan-value pattern, D37): it writes the passed dietaryFilter into the returned MealPlan.dietaryFilter (the snapshot), no DB writes. In `getOrGenerateCurrentWeekPlan()`, REPLACE the hardcoded `getWeeklyItems(weekStart, "none")` at plan-service.ts:118 with `const { dietaryRestriction } = this.preferencesRepository.get();` then `getWeeklyItems(weekStart, dietaryRestriction)` and pass dietaryRestriction into generatePlan(...). getOrGenerateCurrentWeekPlan remains idempotent (returns the existing frozen plan for the current week, plan-service.ts:113-116) — so scenario 5's immutability is reachable via same-week GET /plan; NO regenerate endpoint is needed in increment 1. savePlan persists dietary_filter via the 03-01 repository mapping.

**server.ts**: pass SQLiteUserPreferencesRepository into the PlanService construction.

Deps: 03-04 is REQUIRED (not just 03-03) — scenario 5's third test asserts the LIVE dashboard shows meat again while the snapshot plan hides it, so the live dashboard filter from 03-04 must be working for that contrast assertion to be meaningful. 03-03 for the /settings route used by the scenario preconditions.

TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): the snapshot invariant is a property — for any restriction R snapshotted at generation, subsequent setting changes to R' MUST NOT alter the frozen plan's composition. Where the generatePlan contract supports it, write a fast-check property over (items, R): every meal in the returned plan is isCompatible with R, and plan.dietaryFilter === R, asserted via state-delta over a declared universe (strict=true). Single-example unit tests are FALLBACK only — document why in a `# bypass:` comment if used. The acceptance scenarios (4 and 5) REMAIN single-example E2E through rendered HTML — they verify wiring, and assert only rendered output, never the internal dietary_filter column (Universe guard).

steps.name: Empty-plan warning + link to /settings when no compatible items exist
criteria: Given a vegetarian restriction and a week whose items are all meat/fish (zero compatible), when GET /plan is fetched, then the page shows 'No compatible meals found'
Given the same empty-plan condition, when GET /plan is fetched, then the page contains a direct link with href="/settings"
Given the same condition, when GET /plan is fetched, then neither the meat nor the fish item appears as a meal
steps.scenario_name: @driving_port — Plan view warns and links to settings when no compatible items exist
steps.implementation_notes:
Satisfies acceptance scenario 6 (@driving_port — Plan view warns and links to settings when no compatible items exist). Design §4 (empty-plan warning) + §6 empty-plan-warning row (US-05 scenario-3/4 behavior).

**plan-handler.ts**: in the plan render path, when the generated plan has zero compatible items (the active dietary restriction filters out every seeded item, so no meal references a real discount item), render the message 'No compatible meals found with your current restrictions' plus a direct anchor `<a href="/settings">` so the user can change the restriction. Detect the empty condition from the plan's meals (all discountItemId null / no compatible items), not by re-querying. Thin driving adapter — no business logic; rendering only.

EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: this is server-rendered HTML with exact-string assertions ('No compatible meals found', `href="/settings"`) — a single-shot UI flow. # bypass: rendering test verifies output, not invariants. The compatibility filter that produces the empty set is property-covered upstream (isCompatible at 01-01, plan filtering at 03-05). Integration/E2E (scenario 6) REMAINS single-example.

steps.name: Empty-state discriminator: no-data vs restriction-filtered
criteria: Given an empty discount DB and the default 'none' restriction, when a plan is generated and GET /plan is fetched, then the view shows a no-discounts-available message and does NOT show 'No compatible meals found with your current restrictions'
Given a meat/fish-only DB and a 'vegetarian' restriction (0 compatible), when GET /plan is fetched, then the view still shows 'No compatible meals found' + a link to /settings (scenario 6 unchanged)
steps.scenario_name: @driving_port — Empty plan distinguishes no-data from restriction-filtered
steps.implementation_notes:
Review-driven fix (adversarial review flag: empty-plan no-data/restriction conflation). plan-handler.ts:31-49 currently renders the restriction warning ('No compatible meals found with your current restrictions' + Change-your-dietary-restriction /settings link) for ANY empty plan, including a plan built from an empty DB (fresh install / failed scrape). Fix: branch the empty-state on plan.dietaryFilter (this also makes the snapshot column load-bearing, resolving the 'dietary_filter written-not-read' flag): - hasNoCompatibleItems(plan) AND plan.dietaryFilter === 'none' -> render a NO-DATA message, e.g.

No discounts available this week - please check back after the next catalogue update.

(NO 'change your dietary restriction' steer) - hasNoCompatibleItems(plan) AND plan.dietaryFilter !== 'none' -> keep the existing restriction warning + Change your dietary restriction. New regression AT (add a describe to dietary-preferences.test.ts): seed an EMPTY db (no items), createServer, POST /settings none (default), POST /plan/generate, then GET /plan asserts: (1) contains the no-discounts message text; (2) does NOT contain 'No compatible meals found with your current restrictions'. Reuse the file's existing seed/server helpers (add a seedEmptyDb or just skip seeding). Do NOT modify scenario 6 assertions - they must stay green (vegetarian => dietaryFilter !== 'none' => restriction branch).

steps.name: Harden dietary settings: input validation + snapshot-behavior AT + HTML escaping
criteria: Given POST /settings with an invalid dietary value (e.g. 'banana'), when saved, then the persisted restriction is 'none' (rejected/defaulted) and GET /settings shows 'none' selected; the dashboard behaves as none (all items shown), never as vegan

Given a meat/fish-only DB with vegetarian saved and an (empty) plan generated, when the setting is switched to 'none' and GET /plan is fetched, then the plan STILL shows 'No compatible meals found' (snapshot=vegetarian drives the message), NOT 'No discounts available' — proving the discriminator reads the snapshot, not the live setting
Given a discount item whose name contains HTML special characters, when GET / or GET /plan renders it, then the characters are escaped (no raw <script> injection) — stored-XSS surface closed
steps.scenario_name: @driving_port — Settings input validation, snapshot-drives-message, HTML escaping
steps.implementation_notes: Three review findings folded into one hardening step. D1 (BLOCKER) input validation: settings-handler.ts handlePost currently does `const dietary = (form.get('dietary') ?? 'none') as DietaryRestriction` — the `as` cast is compile-only, so 'banana' persists and isCompatible treats it as vegan-only. Fix: whitelist against ['none','vegetarian','vegan']; on mismatch default to 'none' (do NOT persist garbage). Add an AT: POST /settings dietary=banana -> GET /settings shows none selected AND GET / shows all seeded items (behaves as none, not vegan). D2 (snapshot-behavior AT): add an AT proving the empty-state discriminator reads plan.dietaryFilter (snapshot), not the live setting. Seed meat/fish-only; save vegetarian; POST /plan/generate (empty plan, restriction message); switch setting to none; GET /plan MUST still contain 'No compatible meals found' and MUST NOT contain 'No discounts available'. (Kills the surviving mutant where the discriminator reads the live restriction.) D3 (HTML escaping): create src/shared/html.ts exporting `escapeHtml(s: string): string` (escape & < > " '). Apply to item.name in discount-handler render and meal.name in plan-handler render (the untrusted scraped-data interpolation points). Add an AT: seed an item whose name contains 'x' or similar; assert GET / / GET /plan HTML contains the escaped form and NOT the raw tag. Keep existing item-name assertions green (escaping a plain name like 'Rindersteak' is a no-op). Do NOT weaken any existing assertion. All prior dietary ATs + scenario 6 must stay green.

phases.name: Increment 1.5: Budget cap (warn banner + snapshot, no regenerate)

steps.name: Budget schema: budget_cap_cents on user_settings + meal_plans (guarded migration) + types/repo
criteria: user_settings has a nullable budget_cap_cents column; meal_plans has a nullable budget_cap_cents column
Opening an existing S01-S03 DB (without these columns) does not crash — guarded ALTER TABLE adds them idempotently
UserPreferences gains budgetCapCents?: number|null; MealPlan gains budgetCapCents: number|null; repos read/write both
steps.scenario_name: @driving_port — budget field persists / snapshot immutability (schema prerequisite)
steps.implementation_notes: Add budget_cap_cents (nullable INTEGER) to BOTH user_settings and meal_plans. In db.ts: add to the CREATE_ statements AND add guarded try/catch ALTER TABLE for EACH (two blocks), following the exact dietary_filter/meals idiom (db.ts existing guards). Extend UserPreferences type with budgetCapCents?: number|null (null = no cap). Extend MealPlan with budgetCapCents: number|null. Update SQLiteUserPreferencesRepository get()/upsert() to round-trip budget (default null). Update SQLiteMealPlanRepository save()/findByWeek() to round-trip budgetCapCents. NO regenerate logic. savePlan stays insert-only.

steps.name: Settings budget field: input + euros->cents validation + persistence
criteria: GET /settings renders an <input name="budget"> (euros); after saving a cap it echoes the saved euros value
POST /settings with a valid budget persists budget_cap_cents; invalid (non-numeric, negative) => no cap (NULL), never garbage
Dietary and budget are saved together from the one settings form
steps.scenario_name: @driving_port — Settings exposes a budget field that persists / invalid budget treated as no cap
steps.implementation_notes: Add a budget input to the settings form (name="budget", value in euros, empty = no cap). handlePost: parse euros->cents (euros * 100, rounded). Validation (mirror the dietary whitelist discipline from 03-08): reject non-numeric/negative -> budgetCapCents = null (no cap). Persist via preferencesService.updatePreferences({dietaryRestriction, budgetCapCents}). GET pre-fills the euros value from the saved cents (cents/100), empty when null. Keep the 'Settings saved' confirmation.

steps.name: Budget snapshot at generation + over-budget banner
criteria: generatePlan snapshots the current budget_cap_cents onto the plan; getOrGenerateCurrentWeekPlan reads it from prefs
GET /plan renders data-over-budget iff the plan's snapshotted cap is non-null AND plan.totalSalePrice > cap
No marker when cap null or totalSalePrice <= cap; changing the cap after generation does NOT change the existing plan's banner (snapshot frozen)
steps.scenario_name: @driving_port — over-budget banner appears / no banner under or without cap / snapshot immutability
steps.implementation_notes:
plan-service getOrGenerateCurrentWeekPlan: read budgetCapCents from preferencesRepository.get() and pass into generatePlan; generatePlan sets plan.budgetCapCents (snapshot). plan-handler renderPlanHtml (populated branch): emit a data-over-budget marker (e.g.

...over budget...

) iff plan.budgetCapCents != null AND plan.totalSalePrice > plan.budgetCapCents. Read the SNAPSHOT (plan.budgetCapCents), never the live setting. No banner otherwise. savePlan stays insert-only (one savings_log row/week).

phases.name: SLICE-04: Savings history UI — this-week breakdown + month-to-date + unavailable

steps.name: SavingsService: month-to-date + current-week + unavailable classification (view model)
criteria: Service exposes a summary combining history, current-week breakdown, and a month-to-date sum
Month-to-date sums saved_amount only for weeks whose week_start is in the current calendar month; weeks with totalRegularPrice===0 are excluded
A week with totalRegularPrice===0 is flagged as savings-unavailable
steps.scenario_name: @driving_port — month-to-date total / savings unavailable (aggregation)
steps.implementation_notes: Add a getSummary() (or extend getHistory consumers) returning a view model: { history: SavingsRecord[] (DESC), thisWeek: SavingsRecord|null (weekStart===currentWeekMonday()), monthToDateCents: number, unavailableWeekStarts: string[] }. Month filter: week_start.slice(0,7) === currentWeekMonday().slice(0,7). Exclude any record with totalRegularPrice===0 from monthToDateCents and list it in unavailableWeekStarts. Import currentWeekMonday from src/shared/week.ts. Add colocated unit tests (real sqlite via createDb OR pure over seeded records) covering: month filter excludes prior-month; unavailable week excluded from sum and flagged. Keep getHistory() intact (existing handler/tests depend on it).

steps.name: SavingsHandler: render this-week breakdown + month-to-date + Savings-unavailable
criteria: GET /savings renders data-week-paid / data-week-would-have-paid / data-week-saved for the current week (cents)
GET /savings renders data-month-to-date (cents) = current-month sum excluding unavailable weeks
A week with totalRegularPrice===0 shows the literal 'Savings unavailable' (not €0.00); existing data-saved-amount history rows still render
steps.scenario_name: @driving_port — this-week breakdown / month-to-date / savings unavailable (render)
steps.implementation_notes: Use the service summary. Render a this-week breakdown block with data-week-paid={thisWeek.totalSalePrice}, data-week-would-have-paid={thisWeek.totalRegularPrice}, data-week-saved={thisWeek.savedAmount} (omit the block or render zeros gracefully if thisWeek is null — but ATs seed a current-week row). Render data-month-to-date={monthToDateCents}. In the history rows, when a record.totalRegularPrice===0 render 'Savings unavailable' in place of the euros amount (keep data-saved-amount rows for normal weeks unchanged so the existing savings-handler.test.ts stays green). Escape any interpolated strings via escapeHtml where relevant (week_start is safe, but stay consistent).

steps.name: Savings hardening: this-week unavailable + testable month-to-date + escape store names
criteria: When the current week's totalRegularPrice===0, the this-week breakdown shows 'Savings unavailable' (not €0.00 / data-week-would-have-paid)

Month-to-date summation is a pure function taking an explicit reference-month, unit-tested with a record in the same calendar month as today but a different week-Monday month bucket — proving the intended semantics independently of currentWeekMonday()
Store names in the discount feed are HTML-escaped (stored-XSS closed), consistent with item/meal name escaping
steps.scenario_name: @driving_port — this-week savings unavailable / month-to-date semantics / store-name escaping
steps.implementation_notes:
BLOCKER: savings-handler renderThisWeekBreakdown must branch on isSavingsUnavailable(thisWeek) (totalRegularPrice===0) and render 'Savings unavailable' instead of the paid/would-have-paid/saved fields (mirror the history renderSavedCell branch). Add an AT: seed a CURRENT-week savings_log row with totalRegularPrice=0 -> GET /savings this-week block contains 'Savings unavailable' and NOT data-week-would-have-paid. Keep the normal this-week AT (regular=1420) green.

WARNING (month-to-date testability): extract the month sum into a PURE function in savings-service, e.g. sumMonthToDateCents(records, referenceMonthYYYYMM): sums saved_amount for records whose weekStart.slice(0,7)===referenceMonth AND totalRegularPrice!==0. getSummary derives referenceMonth = currentWeekMonday().slice(0,7) and calls it. Add a colocated UNIT test that calls the pure function with an EXPLICIT referenceMonth (e.g. '2026-08') and records including one at '2026-08-01' and one at '2026-07-27' -> asserts only the 2026-08 one is summed. This breaks the test-blind-spot (test no longer shares the derivation). Also add a one-line clarification to docs/feature/discount-hunt/slices/slice-04-savings-history.md line ~24 that 'current month' = calendar month of the current week's Monday. (Doc note optional but preferred.)

INFO (escape store names): in discount-handler, wrap the interpolated store name at the staleness-warning and store-section <h2> sites in escapeHtml(...). Add/extend an AT (or reuse ui/dietary seed) with a store name containing an HTML special char -> assert escaped form present, raw tag absent. Keep existing 'Aldi Süd' assertions green (escaping a plain name is a no-op).

Do NOT weaken existing assertions; keep getHistory + existing savings-handler.test.ts green; savePlan stays insert-only.

phases.name: UI upgrade: shared layout + inline CSS (clean card-grid) + top nav across all pages

steps.name: Shared page layout helper + inline CSS + top nav
criteria: src/shared/layout.ts exports renderPage({title, activeNav, body}) returning a full HTML document
The document includes a <style> block (clean utility theme) and a top nav (class=site-nav) linking / /plan /savings /settings with the active page marked
renderPage escapes the title and does not alter the provided body markup
steps.scenario_name: @driving_port — shared shell present on every page (layout unit)
steps.implementation_notes: Create renderPage({title, activeNav, body}): returns `<!DOCTYPE html><html lang=en><head>` with charset, viewport meta, <title>{escaped}</title>, and an inline <style> implementing a clean utility theme (light bg, system-ui font, max-width container, a .site-nav top bar with brand 'DiscountHunt' + links, a .card + .card-grid (CSS grid, responsive via repeat(auto-fill,minmax(...))), basic table styling, a .warning/.banner style). Body wrapped in <main class=container>. Nav links: Feed=/ Plan=/plan Savings=/savings Settings=/settings; the link whose key === activeNav gets class=active (or aria-current=page). activeNav is one of 'feed'|'plan'|'savings'|'settings'. Do NOT transform the body string (callers pass ready HTML). Colocated unit test: assert doctype, <style> present, all four hrefs present, active class applied for a given activeNav, title escaped.

steps.name: Route all four handlers through renderPage; feed renders item cards
criteria: GET /, /plan, /settings, /savings each return HTML containing class="site-nav" and links to / /plan /savings /settings and a <style> block
GET / renders each discount item inside a card (data-item-card); existing item-name and staleness/empty-state assertions still pass

All previously-passing assertions (data-estimated-savings, data-meal-slot, data-over-budget, data-saved-amount, data-week-*, data-month-to-date, 'Settings saved', 'No compatible meals found', 'No discounts available', 'Savings unavailable', href=/settings) remain green
steps.scenario_name: @driving_port — nav+shell on all routes; feed uses cards
steps.implementation_notes:
Refactor each handler so it builds ONLY its inner body markup and returns renderPage({title, activeNav, body}). Preserve EVERY existing asserted string/attribute verbatim (data-*, marker texts, hrefs) — the refactor must not drop them. Discount feed: wrap each rendered item in
...
inside a
, keeping escapeHtml(item.name) and existing price/staleness markup. Keep per-store sections + empty-state + staleness warnings intact. Plan/settings/savings keep their tables/forms as the body, now inside the shell. Author tests/acceptance/discount-hunt/ui-shell.test.ts: seed minimal data, start createServer (fresh port range e.g. 5600+random), fetch each of the four GET routes, assert class="site-nav" + the four hrefs + '
